question:
Will G2 Esports win LoL Worlds 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the esports team winning 1st place in the League of Legends 2025 Season World Championship (Worlds 2025).

If the winner of the World Cup 2025 is not determined by December 31, 2025 ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Liquipedia: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.
event_title:
Will G2 Esports win LoL Worlds 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the esports team winning 1st place in the League of Legends 2025 Season World Championship (Worlds 2025).

If the winner of the World Cup 2025 is not determined by December 31, 2025 ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Liquipedia: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.

evidences.title: LoL: All Worlds Winners - Sheep Esports
evidences.content: The 2019 edition was a historic tournament that saw Europe’s G2 Esports dominate the season and raise hopes of a long-awaited Western triumph, only to fall short in the grand final. G2, having reshaped its roster by moving Luka "Perkz" Perković to AD carry to make room for Rasmus "Caps" Winther in the midlane, became an unstoppable force, winning both LEC splits and the Mid-Season Invitational. Entering Worlds as favorites, they navigated a tough bracket, defeating Damwon and SKT to reach the final on home soil in Paris. However, it was FunPlus Phoenix (FPX) that truly shone, despite an unconvincing start in the group stage. Led by the visionary Kim "Doinb" Tae-sang and an extraordinary Gao "Tian" Tian-Liang, FPX dismantled reigning champions Invictus Gaming in the semifinals before [...] The LPL's first ever title at Worlds, after exterminating Fnatic 3-0. Credit: Riot Games. The LPL's first ever title at Worlds, after exterminating Fnatic 3-0. Credit: Riot Games. ## 2019: FunPlus Phoenix FunPlusPhoenix crushed Europe's hope for another year, after deleting G2 Esports 3-0. Credit: Colin Young-Wolff/Riot Games FunPlusPhoenix crushed Europe's hope for another year, after deleting...

evidences.title: G2 Esports - Wikipedia
evidences.content: G2 revamped its roster again for the 2023 LEC season, G2 signed Yike from LDLC OL and Hans Sama from Team Liquid. Mikyx also returned to G2 from Excel Esports. The team took the tournament from 4th place in the regular season, after which they confidently closed the group stage with a score of 2–0 in Group B. G2 Esports won the 2023 LEC Season Finals with a 3–1 victory over Fnatic, marking their second domestic title of the year after their LEC Winter win. As a result, G2 Esports would represent the EMEA region as the first seed at the 2023 League of Legends World Championship. At worlds G2 started off strong with wins against Dplus KIA and Weibo going 2–0 and requiring one more win to play-offs. However G2 would end up losing against Gen.G, NRG Esports and would then be eliminated by [...] G2's League of Legends team competes in the League of Legends EMEA Championship (LEC), the highest level of competitive League of Legends in Europe. The team has won nineteen domestic titles in Europe, the most of any organization, and became the only Western team to win the Mid-Season Invitational in 2019. The organization's Rainbow Six Siege team competes in the European League (EUL), the h...

evidences.title: The Complete History of European League of Legends: From EU LCS to LEC | RFT.GG
evidences.content: ### 2019: Franchising — the LEC is born In November 2018, the EU LCS became the League of Legends European Championship. Relegation was abolished. Ten organizations became permanent partners: Fnatic, G2, Misfits, Schalke 04, Splyce and Vitality stayed — Origen, Excel, Rogue and SK Gaming joined. Year one was a triumph. G2's superteam won both splits, took MSI 2019 and reached the Worlds final. ## 2020–2023: The slot economy With relegation gone, buying a slot became the only way in — and prices exploded. 2020: Splyce became MAD Lions, Origen became Astralis. 2022: Schalke 04 sold its slot to Team BDS. 2023: Misfits sold to Team Heretics; Rogue competed under the KOI name in 2023, before the partnership unraveled and the Rogue brand returned for 2024 Team Heretics 2023 [...] Team Heretics 2023 Photo Credit: Riot Games 2023 also redrew the map. The LEC rebranded from "European" to "EMEA Championship," opening the league to teams and players from the CIS, Turkey and MENA. The calendar moved from two splits to three — Winter, Spring, Summer — plus a Season Finals. ### 2024–2025: Karmine Corp, then NAVI The loudest entrance in LEC history came in 2024: Karmine Corp acquired the slot...

evidences.title: League of Legends World Championship - Wikipedia
evidences.content: Main article: 2024 League of Legends World Championship The 2024 World Championship was held from 25 September to 2 November 2024, with the event taking place in three cities across Europe: Berlin, Paris and London. Twenty-two teams qualified to participate at the World Championship based on placement within their own regional leagues and results gained in this years' Mid-Season Invitational (MSI), with the LCK and LPL's 4th seeds in the Swiss stage reallocated to the winner and a team from the best performing region of the event. During the finals' broadcast of the 2023 World Championship, Riot Games announced that the finals of the 2024 edition of Worlds would take place in London at The O2 Arena on 2 November 2024. [...] Main article: 2019 League of Legends World Championship The 2019 World Championship was held between 2 October to 10 November 2019, in three countries and cities in Europe: Berlin (play-in & groups), Madrid (quarterfinals and semifinals), and Paris (final). Twenty-four teams qualified to participate at the World Championship based on placement within their own regional leagues and previous regional results in international play. [...] Jump to content Wikipedi...

Final selection will be done no later than February 28th, 2025 Total number of teams increased from 30 to 40 teams + 32 spots will be reserved for Open Signups + Up to 8 Direct Invite spots reserved for the Top 8 Teams from the 2024 Club Championship ## Club Championship [edit] [...] However, each Club has to settle on their competing titles and the respective rosters for EWC publicly by May 15th, 2025 - 23:59 AST prior to a set date to be eligible for points in that respective game. This affects signings and acquisitions of new squads or 1on1 players, not minor roster changes in team games, which are regulated separately in each tournament's rulebook. evidences.content: for the following International event on the line, meaning high stakes, all-year round. And yes, you guessed it. this also means we’ll be saying goodbye to Season Finals. Breaking down the splits, in 2025, every single split will see us offer up a unique format. I’ll hand it over to Drakos in due course to provide more details about the Split breakdown for you all. Before I do that though, I want to share two additional changes we're making next year. First up, we've decided to remove Championship Points. Championship points have led to some confusion and discontent in the past, and by removing them, we can make each split more valuable and easier to understand. Secondly, almost all matches will be played on the latest patch whenever possible. This means that the sport will now mirror the [...] This aligns with the three in-game ranked seasons that were announced earlier this year. the LEC and your adventures on the rift are gonna be more close than ever before. Now, EVERY Split will offer up high stakes, with the LEC Trophy AND qualification for the next International event on the line. evidences.title: League of Legends World Championship 2025: Teams, Broadcasts, Schedule, Other Details | Esports Charts
evidences.content: ### When and Where Is LoL Worlds 2025 Taking Place? The League of Legends World Championship makes its long-awaited return to mainland China, marking the country’s first time hosting the event since 2017. Worlds 2025 will be spread across three cities: the Play-In and Swiss Stage in Beijing’s JD Esports Center, the quarterfinals and semifinals at Shanghai’s Mercedes-Benz Arena, and the Grand Final at Chengdu’s Dong’an Lake Sports Park Gymnasium, where a full house is all but guaranteed. The tournament runs from October 14 to November 9, with short breaks scheduled between stages. ### All Teams Qualified for LoL Worlds 2025 [...] ### LoL Worlds 2025 Format and Full Schedule With the ecosystem reshaped, this year’s Play-In looks very different: just two teams and a single best-of-five, replacing the eight-team stage from last year. The winner moves forward, while the loser is knocked out of the tournament. Although the Play-In stage has been trimmed down, it’s set to deliver fireworks, with a brutal showdown between the reigning world champions T1 and Invictus Gaming, who also know what it’s like to lift the Summoner’s Cup. The other major change is the adoption of the Fearless Dr...

evidences.title: Everything YOU Need to Know About Worlds 2025 - YouTube
evidences.content: played in game one are banned for game two. The 20 champions in game 1 and two are banned for game three, and so on. For the Swiss stage, it works the same as it did in 2023 and 2024. The 16 teams are all given a seed based on how they performed in their region, with winners given the one seed, runner-ups the two seed, and third place the three seed. Depending on who wins the playin match, either the LCK or LPL3 seed will be promoted to two seed for this withdraw. This way, there are five one seeds, six two seeds, and five three seeds. In round one, one seeds will be drawn against three seeds, and two seeds will play against other two seeds in a best of one. The way Swiss works is this. If you win a match, you move up to 1 and 0. And if you lose, you go to 0 and one. Every team who has [...] become one to crown the world championship winner. If it works the same way as last year, the 3 and 0 teams will be put into pool one, the 3-1 teams in pool two and the 3 and2 teams in pool three. Then the 3-2 teams will be matched against a 3-0 team with the remaining 3 and 0 team playing against one of the 3-in1 teams. So for quarterfinals, it's better to have finished Swiss stage with the...

evidences.title: The must-watch players of LoL Worlds 2025 - esports.gg
evidences.content: ### SkewMond The LEC’s Rookie of the Year will also make his debut at LoL Worlds 2025. If you’re keeping an eye on up-and-coming talent this year, G2 Esports Rudy “SkewMond” Semaan is the name to watch. The young jungler has impressed fans throughout the season, and he’s ready to turn heads on the world stage. Expect SkewMond to surprise you in more ways than one in the tournament. ### Alvaro [...] ### Chovy On the same roster, we have Jeong “Chovy” Ji-hoon, one of the most prominent mid laners in the world. It’s hard not to highlight Chovy when discussing top talent, since his skill and consistency make him a constant standout. Unlike Ruler, Chovy has yet to win a Worlds title, and this year, he’s more determined than ever to finally lift that long-awaited trophy. ### Peanut Another Korean star to watch is Han “Peanut” Wang-ho. This Worlds event carries special significance for both him and his fans, as it will be his last tournament before beginning mandatory military service. With Peanut set to miss the 2026 season, there’s an emotional weight surrounding his performance this year. evidences.title: PUBG MOBILE 2025 esports updates: PMGO, PMWC, PMGC, and more
evidences.content: ## 2025 PMGC in Thailand The grand finals of the most prestigious PUBG MOBILE tournament, the PUBG MOBILE Global Championship (PMGC), will be held in Thailand for the first time. In previous years, PMGC took place in the UAE (2020), Indonesia (2022), Turkiye (2023), and England (2024). PMGC is PUBG MOBILE’s largest international tournament, featuring 49 teams competing for a $3,000,000 prize pool over five weeks in 2024 PMGC. As the 2025 host country, Thailand will receive one direct invite to the PMGC grand finals. evidences.title: 2025 World Championship format overview
evidences.content: The 2025 World Championship uses a format where the MSI champion qualifies if it advances to regional playoffs. The top three teams besides the MSI champion from each region participate in Swiss Stage, while the 4th seed of the MSI-winning region and the runner-up region start from Play-In. The event features Play-In, Swiss Stage, and a single-elimination Knockout Stage, with a $5,000,000 prize pool.
